title: 10 best herbal teas for jaundice
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jaundice are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the symptoms of this liver disorder by promoting liver detoxification and improving bile flow. The best herbal teas for jaundice include Silybum marianum, Artemisia absinthium, Cynara scolymus, Taraxacum officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Cassia auriculata, Cichorium intybus, and Cassia angustifolia. If left untreated, jaundice can lead to fatigue, nausea, dark urine, and yellowing skin, impacting one's quality of life, daily activities, and overall well-being, ultimately affecting relationships and work productivity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tea, also known as milk thistle tea, is used to treat jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the liver doesn't work properly, causing a buildup of a yellow pigment called bilirubin in the blood. This can be very serious, especially in newborns. Silybum marianum tea contains a compound called silymarin, which helps protect and repair liver cells. When liver cells are damaged, silymarin can help them recover and function properly again. This can reduce the amount of bilirubin in the blood, making jaundice symptoms less severe. Silybum marianum tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the liver. As a result, it can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and improve overall liver function. Silybum marianum tea has been used for centuries to treat liver problems, and its effectiveness has been studied in several scientific trials.

Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a traditional remedy for jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the skin and eyes turn yellow due to a buildup of a yellow pigment called bilirubin in the blood. This buildup can be caused by various factors, including liver disease or bile duct blockages. Glycyrrhiza glabra tea has been used to help reduce inflammation and improve liver function. The active compounds in the tea, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the liver and reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood. By reducing inflammation and improving liver function, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice and promote the body's natural healing processes. Additionally, the tea has been used to support the body's natural detoxification processes, which can help remove toxins and excess bilirubin from the body. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may also help to prevent jaundice from occurring in the first place.

Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, is a plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the liver is not working properly, causing a buildup of bilirubin in the blood, which can turn the skin and eyes yellow. Dandelion tea can help to treat jaundice by supporting the liver's function. The tea is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce liver inflammation and promote liver cell regeneration. Additionally, dandelion tea contains inulin, a type of fiber that can help to stimulate the liver and gallbladder to produce bile, which can help to break down bilirubin and remove it from the body. Drinking dandelion tea may also help to improve digestion, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate fatigue, which are common symptoms of jaundice.

Cichorium intybus tea, also known as dandelion root tea, is a natural remedy that has been used to help with jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the liver is not working properly and bile builds up in the body, causing yellow skin and eyes. Cichorium intybus tea is good for jaundice because it helps to stimulate the liver and improve its function. The roots of the dandelion plant contain compounds that help to break down and remove bile from the body, which can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice. Drinking Cichorium intybus tea can also help to improve digestion and reduce inflammation in the liver, which can further help to alleviate the symptoms of jaundice. Some studies have shown that the flavonoids and terpenoids in Cichorium intybus tea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect the liver from damage and promote healing. Additionally, Cichorium intybus tea is a natural diuretic, which means it can help the body to remove excess fluids and salts that can contribute to jaundice.

Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, is a herb that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. For jaundice, a condition where the liver becomes inflamed and bile builds up in the body, Artemisia absinthium tea may be helpful. The tea is made from the leaves of the wormwood plant, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed, these compounds can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the liver, allowing it to function properly again. Additionally, the tea has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the liver cells from damage and promote healing. By reducing liver inflammation and protecting liver cells, Artemisia absinthium tea may help to alleviate symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and promote a faster recovery. The tea is traditionally consumed as a warm infusion, and its effects may be enhanced when combined with other herbs that support liver health.

Cynara scolymus, also known as artichoke tea, has been used to help with jaundice in some people. The leaves of the artichoke plant contain compounds that may help the liver work better. The liver is the organ that removes waste and makes bile, a yellow liquid that helps digest food. In people with jaundice, the liver doesn't work properly and bile builds up in the body, causing yellow skin and eyes. Drinking artichoke tea may help stimulate the liver to work better, reducing the amount of bile in the body and helping to get rid of the yellow color. Some studies have also shown that artichoke tea may have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the liver from damage and promote healing. Additionally, artichoke tea may also help to improve digestion and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to jaundice.

Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, is a type of spice that has been used for centuries to help with various health problems. When made into a tea, it has been found to be effective in treating jaundice, a condition where the liver is not working properly and causes yellowing of the skin and eyes. This is because turmeric contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin helps to reduce inflammation and improve liver function, which in turn helps to remove the excess bilirubin that causes jaundice. Additionally, turmeric has been shown to stimulate the liver to produce more bile, which helps to break down bilirubin and get rid of it from the body. Drinking turmeric tea has also been found to help improve digestion and reduce nausea, which are common symptoms of jaundice.

Scutellaria baicalensis tea is a herbal remedy that has been used to help people with jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the skin and eyes turn yellow due to a buildup of bilirubin in the body. This tea is made from the root of the Scutellaria baicalensis plant, which is native to Asia. The tea is said to be good for jaundice because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the liver and gallbladder. The tea also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the liver from damage and promote its function. Additionally, Scutellaria baicalensis tea has been shown to improve liver function and increase the production of bile, which helps to remove bilirubin from the body. This can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice, such as yellow skin and eyes, and improve overall liver health.

Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the liver does not work properly, causing a buildup of a yellow pigment in the blood, which can lead to yellowing of the skin and eyes. Peppermint tea is said to aid in jaundice relief because it contains compounds that help to relax the digestive system and improve liver function. The menthol in peppermint tea can also help to reduce inflammation in the liver, which can be a contributing factor to jaundice. Additionally, peppermint tea has been known to stimulate the appetite and improve digestion, both of which are often reduced in people with jaundice. Drinking peppermint tea may also help to reduce the risk of complications associated with jaundice, such as liver damage and scarring. However, it's essential to note that peppermint tea should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment, but rather as a complementary therapy to help manage symptoms.

Bupleurum chinense tea is a traditional Chinese medicine that has been used to help treat jaundice for a long time. Jaundice is a condition where the liver is not working properly and is causing a buildup of yellow pigment in the blood. Bupleurum chinense tea contains a compound called saikosaponin, which is known to have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the liver and improve its function. This can help to break down the yellow pigment and get rid of it from the body. Additionally, Bupleurum chinense tea also has a detoxifying effect on the body, which helps to remove toxins that may be causing the liver to malfunction. When taken as a tea, Bupleurum chinense can help to calm the body and mind, which can also help to relieve the symptoms of jaundice, such as fever and discomfort. The combination of its anti-inflammatory and detoxifying properties makes Bupleurum chinense tea a popular choice for treating jaundice, especially in traditional Chinese medicine.
